title: EUI-64 generation uses hardware ID directly - collision risk across device families
description:
File: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lora_l2.c:67-90

generate_eui64() takes the first 8 bytes of hwinfo_get_device_id() directly. Different MCU families return different formats:
- nRF52: 8-byte unique ID
- STM32: 12-byte UID where bytes 0-3 are lot/wafer (shared across chips)
- ESP32: 6-byte MAC

Taking bytes [0:8] from STM32 UID means bytes 4-7 come from wafer coordinates, which may repeat across production lots.

Trigger: Deploy multiple STM32 nodes from the same production batch.

Bad behavior: EUI-64 collision causes IPv6 address collision. Two nodes respond to same link-local, breaking IPv6 ND and causing intermittent connectivity.

Fix: Hash the full hwid (or at minimum document which MCUs are safe). The comment already notes future: derive from Ed25519 key - prioritize this.
close_reason: Fixed by hashing full hardware ID with SHA-256 before deriving EUI-64. Added TINYCRYPT_SHA256 dependency to LICHEN_LORA_L2 Kconfig. Build verified on nucleo_wl55jc.
